title: Introducing Yourself
definition: Introducing yourself clearly and confidently is an important first step in any conversation, interview, or meeting.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Hi, I don't think we've met. I'm Ananya.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Hi Ananya, I'm Karan. Nice to meet you.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Nice to meet you too. What do you do, Karan?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: I work as a software developer at a tech company. What about you?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: I'm a marketing manager. I've been in this field for five years.

practicePhrases: Hi, I'm [name]. Nice to meet you.
I've been working in this field for [number] years.
In my free time, I enjoy [hobby].
Let me tell you a little about myself.

commonMistakes.why: Singular countable job titles need an article ('a' or 'an').

commonMistakes.wrong: My name is Karan, I am from since Delhi.
commonMistakes.right: My name is Karan. I am from Delhi.
commonMistakes.why: Don't use 'since' with 'from' when stating your hometown.

quiz.question: Which is the correct self-introduction?
quiz.explanation: Use the article 'a' before a singular job title.

quiz.question: I ___ (work) as a teacher for eight years. (present perfect)
quiz.explanation: Use Present Perfect to describe experience continuing to now.
